plan: Create the `LocaleSignals` interface in `src/signals/locale-signals.ts` with typed fields: `timezone: string`, `languages: readonly string[]`, `platform: string`, `doNotTrack: string`

Implement `collectLocaleSignals(): LocaleSignals` pure function in the same file that uses feature detection for each browser API: `Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone`, `navigator.languages ?? [navigator.language]`, `navigator.platform`, and `navigator.doNotTrack ?? globalThis.doNotTrack`
Return safe fallback strings (`'unknown'`, `['unknown']`, `'unknown'`, `'unspecified'`) when any API is unavailable or throws, using try/catch around each collection

Create test file `tests/us-006/locale-signals.test.ts` with unit tests covering: happy-path collection with mocked navigator/Intl globals, fallback values when APIs are missing or undefined, and type-shape validation of the returned object
Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run test` to verify all quality gates pass
testing_recommendations: Unit test that `collectLocaleSignals()` returns an object matching the `LocaleSignals` interface shape (all four keys present, correct types)
Unit test each field individually with mocked browser globals to verify correct extraction from `Intl.DateTimeFormat`, `navigator.languages`, `navigator.platform`, and `navigator.doNotTrack`
Unit test fallback behavior by stubbing each API as `undefined` or throwing, and asserting the safe fallback string is returned
Unit test that `languages` returns a frozen/readonly array copy (not a live reference to `navigator.languages`)
Run `npm run lint`, `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test` as final quality gate
risks: Browser API mocking in Vitest (jsdom/happy-dom) may not perfectly replicate real browser behavior for `Intl.DateTimeFormat` or `navigator` properties — tests should stub at the global level
navigator.platform is deprecated in modern browsers but still widely supported; the fallback handles the eventual removal gracefully
doNotTrack has inconsistent implementations across browsers (some return '1'/'0'/null/undefined) — the collector should normalize but not interpret the raw value
